source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/04_G1717CW 외 7종/90_규격 Test report/1. 신청서 및 제출서류/07. Usability 문서/A-UFER-CYN_Usability Formative Evaluation Report (Rev.0 250525).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Survey-based usability formative evaluation report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents formative assessment intended to filter out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors.
keyPoints: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
GT1717C and GT1717CP differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
The formative evaluation was conducted through a product usability survey with five qualified participants.
All participant survey results were Pass with a score of 4.8, and final decisions were Pass.
The conclusion states that all test results were judged appropriate and product usability was confirmed safe and effective.
Final product usability validity will be confirmed through a comprehensive evaluation.

concepts.name: Formative usability evaluation
concepts.evidence: This report is intended to perform a formative assessment based on various factors including the complexity of the medical device, the potential for hazardous use errors, development schedule, and budget

concepts.name: Hazardous use errors
concepts.evidence: user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors

concepts.name: Survey-based usability assessment
concepts.evidence: Formative evaluation of the product is conducted through a survey on product usability.

concepts.name: Participant qualification
concepts.evidence: The participants must be qualified for the user profile, and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.

concepts.name: Usability test criteria
concepts.evidence: When the results of the questionnaire are converted into a score, the score must be 3 points or higher.

concepts.name: Usability re-evaluation triggers
concepts.evidence: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: A. Changes in the design of the product B. When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

concepts.name: Comprehensive evaluation
concepts.evidence: The validity of the usability of the final product will be confirmed through a comprehensive evaluation.

evidence.claim: The document is identified as Doc No. A-UFER-CYN, Rev. No. 0, issued May 25, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Doc No. A-UFER-CYN Rev. No. 0 Issue date May 25, 2025

evidence.claim: The report title is Usability Formative Evaluation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: 사용적합성 형성 평가 보고서 Usability Formative Evaluation Report

evidence.claim: The evaluated product is a GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product models covered are GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP

evidence.claim: GT1717C and GT1717CP differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: The two products differ only in CsI deposition thickness, as shown below, and are identical in usability. - GT1717C : 300um - GT1717CP : 500um

evidence.claim: The report is intended to perform formative assessment to filter out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is intended to perform a formative assessment based on various factors including the complexity of the medical device, the potential for hazardous use errors, development schedule, and budget as an effective method to filter out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors.

evidence.claim: The formative evaluation method is a survey on product usability using a given scenario.
evidence.evidenceText: Formative evaluation of the product is conducted through a survey on product usability. The survey is conducted by evaluating the survey questions on a given scenario.

evidence.claim: Participants receive the product user manual and training before the survey.
evidence.evidenceText: Before the survey, the participants are provided with a product user manual and training.

evidence.claim: The survey is conducted with five participants qualified for the user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants must be qualified for the user profile, and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.

evidence.claim: The user profile occupation is Doctor or Radiologist.
evidence.evidenceText: Occupation: Doctor or Radiologist

evidence.claim: The user profile qualifications are Medical Licensee or Radiology Licensee.
evidence.evidenceText: Skills and Qualifications: Medical Licensee or Radiology Licensee

evidence.claim: The survey schedule was 2024.10.05 to 2024.10.10.
The questionnaire score acceptance criterion is 3 points or higher.
evidence.evidenceText: When the results of the questionnaire are converted into a score, the score must be 3 points or higher.

evidence.claim: Score conversion uses Very satisfied 5, Satisfied 4, Average 3, Dissatisfied 2, Very dissatisfied 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Very satisfied: 5 points, Satisfied: 4 points, Average: 3 points, Dissatisfied: 2 points, Very dissatisfied: 1 point

evidence.claim: Usability evaluation must be re-evaluated after product design changes or when customer requirements related to usability exist.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: A. Changes in the design of the product B. When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

evidence.claim: All five participants received Pass survey results, a detail score of 4.8, and Pass final decisions.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 Kim*Hyun ... Pass 4.8 Pass ... 2 Moon*Hee ... Pass 4.8 Pass ... 3 Shin*Jin ... Pass 4.8 Pass ... 4 Yoon*Hyun ... Pass 4.8 Pass ... 5 Yoon*Jung ... Pass 4.8 Pass

evidence.claim: The conclusion states that all test results were appropriate and product usability was confirmed safe and effective.
evidence.evidenceText: All test results were judged to be appropriate, and it was confirmed that the usability of this product was safe and effective.

evidence.claim: Final product usability validity will be confirmed through a comprehensive evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: The validity of the usability of the final product will be confirmed through a comprehensive evaluation.

evidence.claim: Key findings included user manual clarity, identifiable buttons and status LED, avoiding component connection confusion, transport ease, clear calibration procedure, patient imaging area markings, and simple maintenance procedure.
evidence.evidenceText: Key Findings: - The user manual must be written in an easy-to-understand manner. - Each button and status LED should be easy to identify. - When connecting components to the detector, there should be no confusion or misconnection. - The product should be easy to transport. - The calibration procedure should be clearly and thoroughly explained. - The detector should have markings identifying the patient's imaging area. - The product maintenance procedure should be simple and concise.

evidence.claim: Implemented improvements included concise user manual wording, LED indicators for Ethernet and power status, prevention of incorrect component connection, detailed connection instructions, clear calibration procedure, patient imaging area markings, and maintenance explanation in the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Improvements Implemented: - The user manual is written in an easy-to-understand and concise manner. - The Ethernet connection status can be confirmed via an indicator (LED). - The power button's LED indicates the power ON/OFF status. - The product is designed to prevent incorrect connection of components, and the user manual contains detailed connection instructions. - The product's calibration procedure is clearly described in the user manual. - The detector has markings that identify the patient's imaging area. How to maintain the product is explained in an easy-to-understand manner in the user manual.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is FDA in source metadata, but the source text does not identify FDA, a jurisdiction, or any regulatory authority.
No standards are cited in the provided source text.
The final page ends with a standalone hyphen after the improvements list, suggesting minor extraction or source formatting noise.
